An observer records a ship’s magnetic bearing as N30°W. At local noon, the magnetic bearing of
the Sun at that place is measured to be 183° 30′. The true bearing of the ship is _____.

Correct answer: D
Solution

(d)

 
Q2 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0
Not attempted

A cement concrete pavement is subjected to a wheel load of 4800 kg. The pavement and material properties are as follows:
• Modulus of elasticity of cement concrete, E = 3 × 105 kg/cm2
• Pavement thickness, h = 15 cm
• Poisson’s ratio of concrete, μ = 0.15
• Modulus of sub grade reaction, k = 5.8 kg/cm3
• Radius of contact area, a = 25.86 cm
Using Westergaard’s equations for wheel load stress, identify the correct statement(s) from the following:

Correct answer: N/A
Solution

(b, c, d)

 
Q3 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0
Not attempted

A base line measured with a steel tape gives an approximate length of 1200 m. The tape is standardized at a pull of 20 kg. The applied pull is 26 kg. The cross-sectional area of the tape is 0.07 cm2 and the modulus of elasticity is 2.11 × 106 kg/cm2. The temperature during measurement is 38°C, while the standard temperature is 18°C. The coefficient of thermal expansion of the tape material is 11.5 × 10–6/°C. The elevation of the base line above mean sea level is 1200 m. Assume the radius of Earth as 6400 km. The total correction (in meters) to be applied to the measured length to determine the correct length of base line at mean sea level is ____. (Answer upto three decimal points)

NAT
Correct answer: 0.099
Solution

0.099 (0.095 - 0.105)

Given the speed-density relationship as u = 55 – 0.44 k, Where:
• u is the speed in km/h
• k is the density in vehicle/km
The maximum flow in vehicles per hour is ______. (Round off to nearest integer)

NAT
Correct answer: 1719
Solution

1719 (1718 - 1720)

 
Q6 Not checked

Not attempted

Vehicles arrive at a toll booth following a Poisson’s distribution at an average rate of 12 vehicles per 10 minutes. What is the probability that at least 2 vehicles arrive in a 1 minute interval?
(Give your answer upto three decimal places).

NAT
Correct answer: 0.338
Solution

0.338 (0.334 - 0.340)

During a reciprocal levelling operation between two points A and B, the following staff readings were observed using one level instrument:

 
 
The R.L. of point A is 550.00 m and distance AB = 950 m
Which of the following options is/are correct?
Correct answer: N/A
Solution

(a, d)

 
Q8 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

List-I contains viscosity grades of bitumen. List-II provides their typical applications.
Match each grade from List I with the appropriate application from List II and choose the correct answer using the codes given below:

 
Correct answer: A
Solution

(a)
(i) VG10 : Spraying applications; paving applications in cold regions in lieu of 80/100 penetration grade.
(ii) VG20 : Paving applications in cold climatic conditions of North India and in high altitude regions.
(iii) VG30 : Paving applications for most part of India, in lieu of 60/70 penetration grade.
(iv) VG40 : Use in high stressed areas like intersections, toll plazas, truck terminals, truck laybyes
in lieu of 30/40 penetration grade.

Q9 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +1 / -0
Not attempted

At a signalised intersection, the cycle time is 90 seconds and the effective green time for a particular movement is 38 seconds. If the saturation headway is 1.2 sec, then the capacity of that lane (in vehicle/hour) is _____. (Give your answer as nearest integer.)

NAT
Correct answer: 1267
Solution

1267 (1266 - 1268)

 
Q10 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

Considering the changes in declination (known as variation), which of the following statements is correct?

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
(i) Diurnal variation : Due to rotation of Earth about its axis.
• More in day time, less in night.
• More in summer, less in winter.
• More at poles, less on equator.
(ii) Annual variation : Due to revolution of Earth about Sun. Generally, observed to be 1-2 minutes.
(iii) Secular variation : Magnetic flux lines swing like a pendulum.
(iv) Irregular variation : Due to magnetic disturbances or storms. Generally, observed in order of 1°.

Q17 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0
Not attempted

To determine the elevation of the base of a watchtower situated on a hill, observations were taken from two instrument stations A and C, placed 100 m apart and being in line with the tower’s location at point B. The vertical angles of elevation to the top of the tower from A and C were observed to be 27°30' and 17°15' respectively. The staff readings on a bench mark of RL 285.5 m were 3.640 m and 2.765 m respectively, when the instruments were stationed at A and C, with the telescope set horizontal.
If the height of the tower above its base is 3 m, then the reduced level of the base of the tower is _____ m. (Answer upto two decimal places).

NAT
Correct answer: 360.90
Solution

360.90 (357 - 363)
